verdict_reasoning: ATG9A is a multi-pass transmembrane protein of the autophagy machinery, residing in Golgi, endosomes, and the phagophore assembly site where it functions as a lipid scramblase. Its TM topology places it in endomembrane compartments, not the plasma membrane outer leaflet. Walking the contextual buckets: (a) no documented cancer/cell-death/activation-induced outer-leaflet ecto-pool for ATG9A specifically; (b) no narrow germline/developmental somatic-lineage surface display; (c) not a lysosomal TM protein with documented lysosomal exocytosis surfacing; (d) no documented PM minority pool or secretory cycling to the surface — trafficking is between Golgi/endosomes/phagophore; (e) no wash-resistant TM-partner tethering on the cell surface. Endomembrane-resident multi-pass TM protein.
predicted_key_uncertainty: ATG9A vesicles traffic through endosomal system; cannot fully exclude a minor undocumented PM pool during autophagy-related membrane dynamics.
